entries.title: Before Attack of the Clones: No Clone Wars

context.label: After The Phantom Menace, before Attack of the Clones

content.fact: Before Attack of the Clones, the Clone Wars have not begun.
content.injection: If context is before AOTC, block Clone Wars military state, Jedi generals, and open Republic-Separatist war unless the story creates a branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After The Phantom Menace, before Attack of the Clones.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Before Revenge of the Sith: No Order 66

context.label: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Before the Order 66 point in Revenge of the Sith, the Jedi Purge has not happened.
content.injection: In pre-Order 66 contexts, do not write Jedi as already purged, exiled, or universally hunted unless the story diverges.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Attack of the Clones, before Revenge of the Sith.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Before A New Hope Destruction: Alderaan Exists

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope

content.fact: Before the Death Star destroys Alderaan, Alderaan still exists as Leia’s homeworld and political base.
content.injection: Before Alderaan’s destruction, do not treat it as already gone; after the Death Star event, do not restore it without an alternate branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Before Empire: No Vader Paternity Reveal to Luke

context.label: After A New Hope, before The Empire Strikes Back

content.fact: Before Bespin in Empire, Luke does not know Vader is his father.
content.injection: Before ESB Bespin, block Luke paternity knowledge; after Bespin, Luke may know Vader’s claim. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After A New Hope, before The Empire Strikes Back.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Before Return: No Leia Sibling Reveal

context.label: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi

content.fact: Before Return of the Jedi, Leia’s status as Luke’s sister is not known in ordinary character knowledge.
content.injection: Before the ROTJ reveal, block sibling-aware behavior between Luke and Leia unless the story explicitly changes the reveal timing.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi.

context.label: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi

content.fact: Before Vader’s ROTJ choice, Vader is not redeemed in active story state.
content.injection: Before ROTJ redemption, write Vader as conflictable but still an Imperial Sith enforcer; do not inject his final redemption as already complete.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After The Empire Strikes Back, before Return of the Jedi.

entries.title: Do Not Import Disney Sequel State into Legends Film Spine

content.fact: The Legends film spine should not assume Disney sequel-era politics, First Order history, or sequel trilogy character outcomes.
content.injection: When this deck is active as Legends film spine, keep Disney sequel continuity out unless a separate Disney canon or crossover deck is deliberately stacked.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episodes I-VI film spine.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: The film spine presents Force use as cinematic, disciplined, and character-limited rather than game-stat scaling.
content.injection: Do not inflate Force powers into unrestricted video-game mechanics when only the film spine is loaded; use conservative cinematic limits unless another deck changes scope.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episodes I-VI film spine.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Rebel Victories Are Costly and Precarious

context.label: After A New Hope, before The Empire Strikes Back

content.fact: Major Rebel victories in the films are strategically important but costly and do not make the Rebellion safe until the Empire is materially disrupted at Endor.
content.injection: Through the OT spine, keep Rebel successes precarious and hard-won rather than turning each victory into immediate strategic dominance.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After A New Hope, before The Empire Strikes Back.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

entries.title: Imperial Authority Depends on Fear and Coercion

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope

content.fact: Imperial authority in the film spine relies on fear, surveillance, military force, and willingness to punish populations.
content.injection: When writing Imperial scenes, emphasize coercive order, chain of command, and intimidation rather than neutral civil administration.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope

content.fact: Between ROTS and ANH, knowledge of surviving Jedi is sparse, dangerous, and often hidden.
content.injection: Do not let ordinary characters casually know where surviving Jedi are after the purge; survivor knowledge should be rare and consequential.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before A New Hope.

entries.title: Sith Master-Apprentice Pattern, Not Public Doctrine

content.fact: The film spine shows Sith master-apprentice succession patterns, but that doctrine is not public galactic knowledge.
content.injection: Use Sith master/apprentice dynamics for Sith-side or informed scenes; do not make ordinary characters fluent in Sith doctrine without cause.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episodes I-VI film spine.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

content.fact: R2-D2 and C-3PO often carry information, messages, or continuity between factions and scenes.

constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episodes I-VI film spine.

entries.title: Romance and Family Knowledge Is Not Public by Default

content.fact: Major film-spine relationships and family truths are often private, secret, or revealed late.
content.injection: Do not treat Anakin/Padme marriage, Luke/Vader paternity, or Luke/Leia sibling truth as public knowledge before their reveal windows.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Episodes I-VI film spine.
